observations.text: Exceptionally detailed phased implementation plan with specific migration files, effort estimates, and engineer allocation

The PostgreSQL database is the critical bottleneck: CPU will saturate within 2-3 months, disk within 3-4 months, and the identified full-table-scan queries will degrade to multi-second response times well before that. The API tier has more headroom but will need horizontal scaling by month 6. This plan prescribes three phases: immediate query/index remediation

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: growth projection calculated (~9x, 50K→446K); database identified as primary bottleneck; 85% disk flagged as critical with 1-2 month timeline; index fix identified as highest-ROI first step; phased plan (indexes→replicas→partitioning/storage); sharding explicitly deferred ('Do NOT start with sharding'); trade_offs include replication lag and cache invalidation complexity (materialized view refresh latency).

observations.text: Exceptionally detailed growth projection with month-by-month numbers and compound math
Disk emergency called out as critical risk with specific 4-6 week timeline
Index fix clearly identified as Week 1 priority with expected impact (<10ms)
Sharding and Elasticsearch explicitly listed in pitfalls_to_avoid for Phase 1
Team capacity (4 engineers) treated as a real constraint throughout the plan
Concrete SQL migration files and infrastructure configs in implementation guidance
Connection pooling risk identified proactively (PgBouncer recommendation)

Read replicas introduced as Phase 1 component rather than medium-term Phase 2, slightly front-loading complexity beyond the rubric's preferred indexes→replicas→storage phasing, though still reasonable
